metadata.summary: Transcribing audio to text using OpenAI Whisper API, local Whisper, or faster-whisper (CTranslate2) with API parameters for timestamps and translation.

content.title: Speech-to-Text Basics
content.summary: Transcribing audio to text using OpenAI Whisper API, local Whisper, or faster-whisper (CTranslate2). Covers API parameters for timestamps and translation, local model selection by hardware, and the 25MB file-size limit that forces pre-splitting.

why.p: Whisper is the de-facto standard for multilingual transcription, but the API is batch-only (no streaming) and has a 25MB file limit. Specifying the language code explicitly saves ~200ms and prevents misidentification between similar languages (Ukrainian/Russian, Serbian/Croatian).

list.item: Transcribing recorded audio: interviews, meetings, podcasts, call recordings
Building voice input pipelines where microphone audio is the primary channel
Generating searchable transcripts with timestamps for media libraries
Multilingual transcription or audio-to-English translation
Pre-processing audio before LLM analysis (summarize, extract, classify)
Real-time streaming requirements below 300ms — use Deepgram or AssemblyAI WebSocket streaming instead
Audio files exceeding 25MB per file — must split first with pydub
Extremely noisy audio (SNR below 5dB) — accuracy degrades to unusable
High-volume production (>10k hours/month) — local faster-whisper on GPU is significantly cheaper

section.name: OpenAI Whisper API
section.p: The Whisper API accepts MP3, MP4, MPEG, MPGA, M4A, WAV, WEBM — not FLAC or OGG. Files must be under 25MB. Use verbose_json with timestamp_granularities for production work.

example.title: Whisper transcription with timestamps
example.code: def transcribe_with_timestamps(audio_path: str) -> dict: with open(audio_path, "rb") as audio_file: response = client.audio.transcriptions.create( model="whisper-1", file=audio_file, response_format="verbose_json", timestamp_granularities=["word", "segment"] ) return { "text": response.text, "segments": response.segments, "words": response.words, "duration": response.duration }
example.note: response.words is a list of Word objects, not dicts. json.dumps() on raw response objects raises TypeError.

antipattern.description: Using response_format other than "verbose_json" when requesting timestamp_granularities=["word"]
antipattern.reason: Non-verbose formats silently drop word timestamps — no error is raised, the timestamps are simply absent from the response.

section.name: Local Whisper
section.p: Local Whisper avoids API costs and the 25MB limit. Model sizing: tiny/base for real-time on CPU; small/medium for quality; large-v3 requires ~10GB RAM and GPU for production throughput.

example.title: LocalWhisper class with device detection

section.name: Faster Whisper
section.p: faster-whisper uses CTranslate2 for 4x speed improvement over original Whisper at the same model size. vad_filter=True removes silence and reduces processing time 20-40% on long files.

example.title: FasterWhisperTranscriber with VAD
example.note: vad_filter=True can silently drop speech segments under 0.5s — disable for audio with single words or short interjections.

rule.statement: Always specify the language parameter explicitly in Whisper calls. Never rely on auto-detection in production.
rule.rationale: Auto-detection adds ~200ms latency and occasionally misidentifies similar languages (Ukrainian/Russian, Serbian/Croatian). Explicit language="uk" eliminates both problems.

rule.statement: Split audio files into chunks under 24MB before uploading to the Whisper API. Use pydub with 10-minute chunks exported as MP3 at 64kbps.
rule.rationale: Whisper API rejects files over 25MB. A 90-minute meeting WAV typically exceeds this. Chunking at 10 minutes leaves margin for encoding overhead.

example.title: Audio file splitting with pydub
example.code:
from pydub import AudioSegment import math, os
def split_audio(path: str, chunk_min: int = 10, out_dir: str = "/tmp") -> list[str]: audio = AudioSegment.from_file(path) chunk_ms = chunk_min * 60 * 1000 n = math.ceil(len(audio) / chunk_ms) paths = [] for i in range(n): chunk = audio[i * chunk_ms:(i + 1) * chunk_ms] p = os.path.join(out_dir, f"chunk_{i:03d}.mp3") chunk.export(p, format="mp3", bitrate="64k") paths.append(p) return paths

rule.statement: Pre-process audio before transcription: convert to 16kHz mono WAV, normalize amplitude, apply noise reduction. Improves accuracy 5-15%.
rule.rationale: Whisper was trained on varied audio but performs best on clean 16kHz mono. Format conversion with ffmpeg before API upload is a low-cost accuracy improvement.

rule.statement: Cache transcriptions by SHA-256 hash of the audio file bytes. Never re-transcribe the same file.
rule.rationale: Re-transcribing the same file wastes API cost and time. Content-based hashing catches identical files with different names.

rule.statement: Use the Whisper prompt parameter to include domain-specific terms, speaker names, and expected vocabulary — keep it under 100 tokens.
rule.rationale: The prompt parameter is prepended to model context (not searched against audio), reducing substitution errors for domain terms. Over 100 tokens dilutes the effect.

rule.statement: Use vad_filter=True in faster-whisper for long files with silence — reduces processing time by 20-40%.
rule.rationale: Voice Activity Detection removes silence segments before processing, saving computation. However, it can drop short speech segments under 0.5s.

rule.statement: Use Deepgram or AssemblyAI WebSocket streaming for real-time requirements below 300ms. Use faster-whisper locally for high-volume (over 10k hours/month).
rule.rationale: Whisper API is batch-only — it requires a complete file upload. Streaming STT providers process audio frames as they arrive. Local faster-whisper is significantly cheaper at scale.

antipattern.description: Polling AssemblyAI or AWS Transcribe with a synchronous await
antipattern.reason: Long audio transcription jobs via cloud services are async — agent must poll a status URL. Synchronous waiting blocks the event loop for minutes.

section.name: Agentic Workflow
section.p: STT fits into agent pipelines as a preprocessing node: agent receives an audio file path or URL, calls the transcription function, and passes the text to downstream LLM processing (summarization, extraction, classification). Claude subagents can orchestrate the full pipeline: split long audio → transcribe chunks → merge transcripts → analyze. The STT step itself is a single tool call with no LLM reasoning required — use it as a primitive in tool definitions.

list.item: Custom audio pipeline agent — given a media file, split by silence, transcribe each chunk, merge with timestamps, return full transcript
/faion — scaffold a transcript processing pipeline (meeting → action items, podcast → summary)
Whisper API accepts MP3, MP4, MPEG, MPGA, M4A, WAV, WEBM — not FLAC or OGG; convert before upload
The prompt parameter is prepended to the model context, not searched against the audio — keep it under 100 tokens
response.words from timestamp_granularities=["word"] is a list of Word objects, not dicts — agents using json.dumps() on raw response objects will get TypeError
Local Whisper on CPU with large-v3 model requires ~10GB RAM; agents running on memory-constrained hosts should use base or small
Transcription of code-switching audio (mixed languages per sentence) requires language=None + post-processing — explicit language setting forces a single language
Long transcription jobs via AssemblyAI/AWS are async — agent must poll status URL, not await synchronously

template.purpose: FasterWhisperTranscriber with VAD filter and streaming
pydub helper to split audio into chunks under 24MB

section.name: Services and Tools
section.p: CLI tools for local development and deployment:

list.item: openai — Whisper API: transcription + translation; install with pip install openai
openai-whisper — Local Whisper model (original); install with pip install openai-whisper
faster-whisper — CTranslate2-optimized local Whisper; 4x faster; install with pip install faster-whisper
whisperx — faster-whisper + forced alignment + diarization; install with pip install whisperx
pydub — Audio splitting, format conversion, silence detection; install with pip install pydub
ffmpeg — Audio decoding, resampling, format conversion; system install: apt install ffmpeg
deepgram-sdk — Real-time streaming STT + diarization; install with pip install deepgram-sdk
OpenAI Whisper API (SaaS) — Yes, SDK available. $0.006/min; 25MB limit; no streaming; 99 languages
Deepgram Nova-2 (SaaS) — Yes, WebSocket/REST. Real-time + batch; speaker diarization; $0.0043/min
AssemblyAI (SaaS) — Yes, SDK. Async batch + streaming; LeMUR for LLM-on-transcript
AWS Transcribe (SaaS) — Yes, Boto3. Batch + streaming; custom vocabulary; PII redaction
Google Speech-to-Text (SaaS) — Yes, Python client. Real-time streaming; 125 languages; telephony model
Azure Speech (SaaS) — Yes, SDK. Real-time + batch; speaker diarization; custom models
faster-whisper (OSS) — Yes, Python. Self-hosted; best cost/quality for high volume
